linkedin.preferred_format: hook line → 3 bullet points → call to action → hashtags

content_cadence.release: immediately after release metadata promotes and deployment evidence exists
content_cadence.security_fix: immediately after the fix ships — lead with the CVE or control name
content_cadence.feature: after user-visible behavior is confirmed in currentRelease
content_cadence.progress: twice per week while active beta work is shipping
content_cadence.cicd_milestone: when a meaningful pipeline or SDLC gate is completed

posting_rules: Do not claim a feature is live unless it appears in currentRelease or the public changelog.
Do not claim a patch is shipped until release metadata promotes it and deployment evidence exists.
X posts can be up to 25,000 characters (verified account). URLs count as 23 characters. Keep posts concise for engagement — under 500 chars is ideal.
LinkedIn posts must be factually grounded — use highlights and trust signals from release-metadata.json.
approved_by must be set before moving any file from drafts/ to queue/.
The bot must never post a file with status draft — only ready.
Do not paste internal evidence paths or report filenames verbatim into public posts.

bot_instructions.poll_strategy: read all .json files in queue/ where status == ready
bot_instructions.after_post: set post_id, published_at on the platform object, set top-level status to published, move file to published/
bot_instructions.on_error: leave file in queue/, set platform status to error, log reason
